title: Sleep
lead: Sleep isn't a luxury — it's when your mind and body repair. When it breaks down, everything gets harder. The good news: sleep responds well to small, consistent changes.
explanation: Sleep problems can mean trouble falling asleep, staying asleep, waking too early, or sleep that never feels restful. Almost everyone struggles at some point, especially during stress, anxiety, grief, or big life changes.

A frustrating loop often forms: you can't sleep, so you worry about not sleeping, and that worry keeps you awake. Trying harder to sleep tends to backfire, because sleep is something you allow, not something you force.

The most effective long-term help for ongoing insomnia isn't medication but a structured approach called CBT-I (cognitive behavioural therapy for insomnia), which retrains your sleep patterns and calms the anxious relationship with sleep.
symptoms: Waking during the night or too early
Lying awake with a racing mind
Relying on screens or substances to sleep

immediate.title: Get out of bed if you can't sleep
immediate.description: If you're awake and frustrated after ~20 minutes, get up, keep lights low, and do something calm until sleepy. This stops your brain from associating bed with wakefulness.

immediate.title: Slow breathing to wind down
steps: Lie comfortably and breathe out fully
Breathe in through your nose for 4
Let each exhale soften your body a little more

immediate.title: Offload the mind
immediate.description: Keep a notepad by the bed. If thoughts or to-dos keep surfacing, write them down so your brain can let go of holding them.

longTerm.title: Protect a consistent rhythm
longTerm.description: Wake at the same time daily (even after a bad night), get morning daylight, and keep the bedroom cool, dark, and screen-free. Consistency resets your body clock.

longTerm.title: CBT-I
longTerm.description: The gold-standard treatment for chronic insomnia. Free CBT-I guides and apps can retrain your sleep without medication.

longTerm.title: Address what's underneath
longTerm.description: Persistent sleep problems are often driven by stress, anxiety, or low mood. Tending to those directly often improves sleep too.

resources.title: Insomnia — self-help and treatment
resources.whyHere: Clear, trustworthy guidance on causes and what actually helps.

resources.title: Healthy sleep tips
resources.whyHere: Evidence-based sleep hygiene guidance, free to read.

resources.title: CBT-i Coach
resources.whyHere: Free, evidence-based app built around CBT for insomnia.

resources.title: NSDR / Yoga Nidra for rest
resources.whyHere: Non-sleep deep rest practices that calm the nervous system before bed.

matchPhrases: i keep waking up at night
